channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Filthy street, 22nd July
item.description:
Once again, Bostall Manorway looks absolutely disgusting. There is rubbish everywhere. Cans, plastic bottles, glass bottles, food wrappers. It is not safe with all the broken glass around.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bostall Manorway, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Filthy Street, 22nd July
item.description:
The amount of rubbish in the gutters along Abbey Wood Road is not acceptable. It is particularly bad at the junction with Bostall Manorway, around the convenience store. I'm guessing your response will be that it will be done in the normal rotation, but this is clearly not regular enough, as rubbish quickly builds up again. It's a danger to children and dogs with all the broken glass around.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Abbey Wood Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Estate gate locking mechanism broken, 22nd July
item.description:
The gate to this estate has been broken again with the lock mechanism completely removed. Gate is now unlocked and can be accessed by non residents for parking which happens and overwhelms the estate
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mapledene Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping outside number 37/39, 22nd July
item.description:
My neighbours have had building works and a skip was sited, which has since been removed. However, items were fly tipped NEXT to the skip and it appears that other items have been deposited since its removal.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Highclere Street, Perry Vale, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped furniture, 22nd July
item.description:
Flytipped furniture left alongside homeless wheelie bins at top of Elfindale Road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Elfindale Road, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damaged floor in playground - trip hazard, 22nd July
item.description:
Damaged/gapped safety surfacing at playground behind Finnewcane Drive, Bromley, BR5 4ED. The rubber safety matting around the play equipment and swings has pulled away from the concrete edging in several places, leaving gaps of several centimetres between the surfacing and the path. Weeds are growing through the gaps and the rubber edge is fraying, creating a trip hazard. My son and other children have had some fairly bad falls here because of it. See attached photos showing the separated edging around the climbing frame and the swing area. Please could this be inspected and the edging/surfacing repaired as a priority given the injury risk.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stanton Close, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped., 22nd July
item.description:
On grass verge.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Pinewood Avenue, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Basal branches of street tree extending into carriageway, scraping the 358 bus sides and obscuring the view of approaching buses from the bus shelter. Street tree fronting 409 Upper Elmers End Rd, Beckenham, 22nd July
item.description:
Basal branches of street tree extending into carriageway, scraping the 358 bus sides and obscuring the view of approaching buses from the bus shelter. Street tree fronting 409 Upper Elmers End Rd, Beckenham
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Upper Elmers End Road, Upper Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped broken wheelie bin, full, 22nd July
item.description:
Broken wheelie bin full of rubbish dumped at side of road. Doesn't belong to any nearby residents.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rolleston Avenue, Crofton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Skip, 22nd July
item.description:
Skip on road no lights
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kingsdown Way, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No access to west side of Downsbridge Road, 22nd July
item.description:
You cannot go down Downs bridge Road towards Albemarle Road without crossing from the west side to the east because the west side has been blocked off by the builders working on the new apartments on the corner. It is hazardous crossing Downs Bridge Road specially if you have a pram or have mobility issues because of the proximity of the roundabout to where you have to cross and the amount of traffic going down the road. Anyone wanting to go westwards down Albemarle Road has to cross Downs Bridge Road twice.Cars parked on both sides of the road means that there is no room for two cars passing in opposite directions frequently resulting in cars being banked up behind each other. Most of the construction work appears to have been completed and there does not appear to have been any big vehicles entering the site for some time. It should surely be possible now to lay down some sort of pavement cover that would allow pedestrians to walk down the west side of Downs Bridge Road. We have been inconvenienced for several years now.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albemarle Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Full and overflowing litter bin in Alexandra Rec, in the outer lawn adjoining the enclosed children's play are, about opposite 17, Maitland Rd., 22nd July
item.description:
Full and overflowing litter bin in Alexandra Rec, in the outer lawn adjoining the enclosed children's play are, about opposite 17, Maitland Rd. This may be a hangover from the open air music and stalls on Sunday, or just more people, including maybe a, school, using the park this, afternoon.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Maitland Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Old bench left on corner which is obstructing the pavement., 22nd July
item.description:
Someone has left an old bench on the corner of my house. Not only is it a hazard on the pavement but if used by passer-by could cause injury if sat on.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 38, Middleton Avenue, Albany Park, Foots Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unwanted Vacuum, Future, and Carpet Dumped on Pavement, 22nd July
item.description:
An unwanted vacuum, furniture, and carpet have been left dumped on the pavement. Can the local team please clear it away. Thank you.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Villas Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Various items of Rubbish Dumped on Path, 22nd July
item.description:
Various items of rubbish have been left dumped on the path. Can the local team please clear it away. Thank you.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Arthur Grove, Plumstead

item.title: Unwanted Furniture Dumped on Path, 22nd July
item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Burrage Road, Plumstead

item.title: Unwanted Timber Dumped on Pavement, 22nd July
item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Griffin Road, Plumstead

item.title: Blackberry branble bush over grown, 22nd July
item.description:
Large Thorny blackberry bush obstructing the pavement by being overgrown and hanging down
